title: Splicing Fidelity
text:
The {out}_perind.counts.noise.gz noise count file has these main features:

noisy coordinates are the min(starts) and max(ends) of all functional introns’ coordinates within each cluster
reads are summed up

Effectively, it indicates for each cluster the number of reads attributed to noisy splicing (by sample)


Code
split_chrom = function(chrom_v) {
  chrom_v = str_split(chrom_v, ":") %>% 
  map(~ list(chrom = .x[1], 
             start = .x[2], 
             end = .x[3],
             strand = str_extract(.x[4], "[\\+\\-]"),
             clu = str_extract(.x[4], "clu_[0-9]+"),
             noisy = str_detect(.x[4], "\\*")
             )) %>% 
  map(as.data.table) %>% 
  rbindlist()
  return(chrom_v) # data.table
}




Code
noisy2 = noisy[, c(split_chrom(chrom), .SD), .SDcols = patterns("^ERR")] %>% 
  melt(measure.vars = patterns("^ERR.+"), 
       variable.name = "samp", 
       value.name = "frac"
       ) %>% 
  .[, `:=`(
    num = str_split(frac, "/", simplify = T)[, 1] %>% as.integer,
    denom = str_split(frac, "/", simplify = T)[, 2] %>% as.integer
  )]
noisy2[, frac := num/denom]


Number of clusters having legit noisy introns 29690


Code
noisy2[noisy & num > 2 & denom > 30 & frac > 0 & frac < 1, clu] %>% unique %>% length


[1] 29690


Number of samples having noisy splicing?


Code
noisy2[noisy & num > 2 & denom > 30 & frac > 0 & frac < 1,] %>% 
  .[, .(n_samp = length(unique(samp))), by = clu] %>% 
  ggplot() + stat_ecdf(aes(n_samp)) + 
    geom_hline(yintercept = .8, color = "red", linetype = "dashed")





ecdf of number of samples detected per noisy splicing event




Do some samples have more noisy splicing?


Code
noisy2[noisy & num > 2 & denom > 30 & frac > 0 & frac < 1,]  %>% 
  .[, .(n_noisy_clu = length(unique(clu))), by = samp] %>% 
  .[, 
    .(samp = str_split(samp, "\\.", simplify = T)[, 1], 
      n_noisy_clu)
  ] %>% 
  ggplot() + stat_ecdf(aes(n_noisy_clu)) + 
    geom_hline(yintercept = .8, color = 'red', linetype = "dashed")





ecdf of number of noisy splicing events per sample






Code
noisy2[noisy & num > 2 & denom > 30 & frac > 0 & frac < 1,]  %>% 
  .[, .(n_noisy_clu = length(unique(clu))), by = samp] %>% 
  .[, 
    .(samp = str_split(samp, "\\.", simplify = T)[, 1], 
      n_noisy_clu,
      grp = cut_interval(n_noisy_clu, length = 3000, dig.lab = 6))
  ] %>% 
  ggplot() + geom_boxplot(aes(grp, n_noisy_clu)) + 
    geom_point(aes(grp, n_noisy_clu, color = samp), position = position_jitter(width = .2, height = .2)) + 
    theme(legend.position = "None")





Number of splicing events detected per sample




min: 2522, max 17223 noisy clusters per sample.


Code
noisy2[noisy & num > 2 & denom > 30 & frac > 0 & frac < 1,]  %>% 
  .[, .(n_noisy_clu = length(unique(clu))), by = samp] %>% 
  .[, 
    .(samp = str_split(samp, "\\.", simplify = T)[, 1], 
      n_noisy_clu)
  ] %>% 
  .[order(-n_noisy_clu)]


          samp n_noisy_clu
  1: ERR188427       17223
  2: ERR188432       14913
  3: ERR188395       14798
  4: ERR188063       14484
  5: ERR188084       14443
 ---                      
448: ERR188190        3715
449: ERR188148        3599
450: ERR188280        3261
451: ERR188350        3161
452: ERR188079        2522

title: Summarize Geuvadis samples
text:
Common samples between Geuvadis and 1000 genome project genotypes
Sample IDs, aka. HG##### or NA##### numbers are common keys that link samples in Geuvadis and 1000 genome project.
Below table shows number of sampels in Geuvadis and 1KGP as well as the number of samples that can be linked.


Code
# get all genotype sample names
cmd = "  view -h /project2/yangili1/zpmu/1kg_b38/CCDG_14151_B01_GRM_WGS_2020-08-05_chr1.filtered.shapeit2-duohmm-phased.vcf.gz | awk '$1 ~ /#CHROM/'"

SamIDs_1kgp = system2("bcftools", cmd, stdout = T) %>% 
  scan(text = ., what = character())
SamIDs_1kgp = SamIDs_1kgp[10:length(SamIDs_1kgp)]

# samples that can be linked between Geuvadis and 1KGP
SamIDs_common = intersect(SamIDs_1kgp, geuvadis_meta$Sample)






N




Geuvadis Samples
464


1KGP Phase 3 Samples
3202


Samples in both
451





Focusing on common samples


Code
# geuvadis linkable samples that only have 1 ERR IDs
geuvadis_singleERR_samples = geuvadis_meta[Sample %in% SamIDs_common, .(Sample, run_id)] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  .[, .(N_ERR = length(unique(run_id))), by = Sample] %>%
  .[N_ERR == 1, Sample]


Out of all 464 Geuvadis samples, 280 of them has 1-to-1 ERR IDs. The other samples have multiple ERRs per sample.
For my test analysis, I am running only on these 1-to-1 samples to establish analysis pipeline, but later on formal analysis will include all linkable samples.


Saving sample ids to files
Save all linkable Geuvadis sample ids to a text file, with 1 ID per row.


Code
#write_lines(SamIDs_common, "../code/resources/geuvadis-1kgp-common-sample-id.txt")


Save all 1-to-1 linkable Geuvadis sample ids to a text file, 1 ID per row.


Code
#write_lines(geuvadis_singleERR_samples, "../code/resources/geuvadis-1kgp-common-1to1only-sample-id.txt")




Check junction file samples from Ankeeta


Code
noise = fread("../code/results/Geuvadis/noise/Geuvadis_perind.counts.noise.gz",
              colClasses = 'character', nrows = 5)
noise_ERRs = names(noise)
noise_ERRs = noise_ERRs[noise_ERRs != "chrom"]
noise_ERRs = str_extract(noise_ERRs, "ERR[0-9]{5,7}")
noise_samples = filter(geuvadis_meta, run_id %in% noise_ERRs) %>% 
  pull(Sample) %>%
  unique


These samples in Ankeeta’s dataset do not have genotype in 1KGP


Code
setdiff(noise_samples, SamIDs_1kgp)


character(0)


Ankeeta’s dataset has 452 ERR IDs, smaller than the full Geuvadis dataset (667). 37% of samples have more than 1 runs.


Code
filter(geuvadis_meta, Sample %in% noise_samples) %>% 
  select(Sample, run_id, Pop_id) %>% 
  group_by(Sample) %>% 
  mutate(N = length(unique(run_id))) %>% 
  mutate(morethan1runs = N > 1) %>% 
  ungroup %>% group_by(morethan1runs) %>% 
  summarise(N_samples = length(unique(Sample)))


# A tibble: 1 × 2
  morethan1runs N_samples
  <lgl>             <int>
1 FALSE               274

title: Genotype PCA results
text:
Code
#Geuvadis genotype PCA

pca_file = dir("../code/results/Genotype/Geuvadis", ".+geno.+pca$", full.names = T)
pca_file = naturalsort::naturalsort(pca_file)
names(pca_file) = str_extract(pca_file, "chr[0-9]{1,2}")

pca = map(pca_file, ~ fread(.x))

walk(pca, ~.x[, PC := str_extract(SampleID, "PC[0-9]{1,3}$")])
pca = map(pca, ~.x[, -c("SampleID")])
pca = map(pca, ~ column_to_rownames(.x, "PC") %>% t %>% as.data.frame)
pca = map(pca, 
          ~ left_join(x = rownames_to_column(.x, "Sample"), 
                      y = metadata[, c("Sample", "Pop_id")], 
                      by = "Sample")
)



Are the column orders the same for each chromosome?
The answer is yes. See below:


Code
chr1_cols = pca$chr1 %>% names
map_lgl(pca, ~ all(chr1_cols == names(.x)))


 chr1  chr2  chr3  chr4  chr5  chr6  chr7  chr8  chr9 chr10 chr11 chr12 chr13 chr14 chr15 chr16 chr17 chr18 chr19 chr20 chr21 chr22 
 TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE 




Code
ggplot(pca$chr1) + 
  geom_point(aes(PC2, PC1, color = Pop_id))







Code
ggplot(pca$chr1) + 
  geom_point(aes(PC4, PC3, color = Pop_id))







Code
ggplot(pca$chr1) + 
  geom_point(aes(PC5, PC6, color = Pop_id))

title: Geuvadis noisy splicing QTL
text:
qqplots - qtltools adjusted p-values
These p-values are adjusted for number of variants, but not adjusted for phenotypes.

chr1 - chr5chr6 - chr10chr11 - chr15chr16 - chr20chr16 - chr20




Code
leafcutter::multiqq(
  list(chr1 = qtls$chr1$pval_emp,
       chr2 = qtls$chr2$pval_emp,
       chr3 = qtls$chr3$pval_emp,
       chr4 = qtls$chr4$pval_emp,
       chr5 = qtls$chr5$pval_emp
       )
)









Code
leafcutter::multiqq(
  list(chr6 = qtls$chr6$pval_emp,
       chr7 = qtls$chr7$pval_emp,
       chr8 = qtls$chr8$pval_emp,

       chr10 = qtls$chr10$pval_emp
       )
)

  list(chr11 = qtls$chr11$pval_emp,

       chr15 = qtls$chr15$pval_emp

  list(chr16 = qtls$chr16$pval_emp,
       chr17 = qtls$chr17$pval_emp,

       chr20 = qtls$chr20$pval_emp

)









Code
leafcutter::multiqq(
  list(
       chr21 = qtls$chr21$pval_emp,
       chr22 = qtls$chr22$pval_emp
       )
)










How many qtls passing qval < 0.1?


Code
puta_qtls = map(qtls, ~ .x[qval < .1]) %>% 
  rbindlist


Total number of qtls: 1322


Code
chroms = forcats::as_factor(paste("chr", 1:22, sep=""))
puta_qtls[, .(n_qtls = length(unique(pid))), by = pchr] %>% 
  .[, .(qtls = n_qtls, chr = factor(pchr, chroms))] %>% 
  ggplot() + geom_col(aes(chr, qtls)) +
    labs(x = NULL) + 
    theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30))







Code
# load in annotation
anno.gene = "../../genome_index/hs38/gencode.v38.primary_assembly.annotation.dataframe.csv"
mycols = c("seqname", "start", "end", "strand", "gene_name")
anno.gene = fread(anno.gene) 
anno.gene = anno.gene[gene_type %in% c("protein_coding") & 
                        feature %in% c("gene") & 
                        source %in% c("HAVANA"), ..mycols] %>% 
  unique %>% 
  makeGRangesFromDataFrame(keep.extra.columns = T)

# remove scaffolds
chroms = paste("chr", 1:22, sep = "")
anno.gene = anno.gene[seqnames(anno.gene) %in% chroms]




Code
# annotate pid with gene names
puta_loci = puta_qtls[, .(pid, pchr, pstart, pend, pstrand)] %>% 
  makeGRangesFromDataFrame(keep.extra.columns = T, 
                           seqnames.field = "pchr", 
                           start.field = "pstart", 
                           end.field = "pend",
                           strand.field = "pstrand")

hits = GenomicRanges::findOverlaps(puta_loci, anno.gene, minoverlap = 1)
hits = cbind(puta_loci[queryHits(hits)] %>% mcols,
                 anno.gene[subjectHits(hits)] %>% mcols
                 )
hits = as.data.table(hits) %>% 
  .[,  list(genes = pull(.SD, gene_name) %>% paste0(collapse = ",")), by = "pid"]

puta_qtls = hits[puta_qtls, on = "pid", nomatch = NA]




Code
puta_qtls[!is.na(genes)][order(qval)]

title: Phenotype PCA results
text:
Code
#Geuvadis genotype PCA

pca_file = dir("../code/results/Geuvadis/noise_pheno", ".+_pheno.+pca$", full.names = T)
pca_file = naturalsort::naturalsort(pca_file)
names(pca_file) = str_extract(pca_file, "chr[0-9]{1,2}")

pca = map(pca_file, ~ fread(.x))

pca = map(pca, ~ column_to_rownames(.x, "SampleID") %>% t %>% as.data.frame)
pca = map(pca, 
          ~ left_join(x = rownames_to_column(.x, "Sample"), 
                      y = metadata[, c("Sample", "Pop_id")], 
                      by = "Sample")
)



Are the samples in the same order?


Code
chr1_samples = pca$chr1$Sample
map_lgl(pca, ~ all(chr1_samples == .x$Sample))


 chr1  chr2  chr3  chr4  chr5  chr6  chr7  chr8  chr9 chr10 chr11 chr12 chr13 chr14 chr15 chr16 chr17 chr18 chr19 chr20 chr21 chr22 
 TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE  TRUE 




Are the in the same order as genotype?


Code
geno_pca_chr1 = "../code/results/Genotype/Geuvadis/chr1_geno.pca"
geno_pca_chr1 = fread(geno_pca_chr1)
geno_samples = names(geno_pca_chr1)
geno_samples = geno_samples[str_detect(geno_samples, "[A-Z]{2}[0-9]{5,7}")]




Code
ggplot(pca$chr1) + 
  geom_point(aes(PC2, PC1, color = Pop_id))







Code
ggplot(pca$chr1) + 
  geom_point(aes(PC4, PC3, color = Pop_id))







Code
ggplot(pca$chr10) + 
  geom_point(aes(PC5, PC6, color = Pop_id))

title: Validate GTEx V8 junction file
text:
Downloaded file path:

resources/GTEx/juncs/GTEx_Analysis_2017-06-05_v8_STARv2.5.3a_junctions.gct.gz

Because the file is rather large, a python script is used to compute the histogram of each junction.

workflow/scripts/make_histogram_of_GTEx_juncs.py


The GTEx V8 junction file include low reads
See plots below. It seems this data set does not filter out


Code
# histograms for each junction
hist_data = fread("../code/resources/GTEx/juncs/GTEx_Analysis_2017-06-05_v8_STARv2.5.3a_junctions_histogram.txt")
# junction coordinates and gene id
col_data = fread("../code/resources/GTEx/juncs/GTEx_Analysis_2017-06-05_v8_STARv2.5.3a_junctions_names.txt", col.names = c("junc", "gene_id"))



Samples with 0-5 readsDistribution of Min(reads)




Code
ggplot(hist_data) + geom_histogram(aes(`bin_0-5`), bins=20) +
  labs(x = "Num. of samples with <5 reads", y = NULL)









Code
ggplot(hist_data) + stat_ecdf(aes(Min), bins = 20) +
  labs(x = "minimum reads", y = NULL)


Warning: Ignoring unknown parameters: bins

title: Splicing Fidelity
